comment: Declarations that the unused-exports CI check should not flag. Each entry pairs a fully-qualified name with the reason it is exempt; `check_unused_exports` errors out if a listed name does not exist in the loaded environment, which keeps the file from going stale silently.

exemptions.name: Core.Dsl.IdentRefs.dslProofMarker
exemptions.reason: Identity-function placeholder emitted by the DSL into rendered Lean source. `graftDslProofMarkers` rewrites each `dslProofMarker (…)` placeholder back to the user's original `proof[…]` body before elaboration, so no Lean constant referrer is ever stored in the env; the function exists only because the placeholder must parse as a term.

exemptions.name: Core.Dsl.IdentRefs.resolveStructField
exemptions.reason: Struct-field resolver invoked from `run_cmd` smoke tests in `Tests/DslGotoDef.lean`. `run_cmd` blocks execute at elaboration time but do not produce stored Lean constants, so their references are invisible to `getUsedConstantsAsSet`. The production `↦` parser rule no longer calls this resolver (gotoDef goes through `recordStructFieldRef` + `graftLocalIdentsFromBuffers`), but the function still backs the regression tests that pin the registry's behaviour.

exemptions.name: DslExprF.embed
exemptions.reason: Auto-generated by the `derive_base_functor` macro as part of the standard recursion-scheme API (dual of `DslExpr.project`). The macro emits the full set of combinators for symmetry; this codebase consumes `cata` / `para` and their monadic variants, but `embed` is part of the published interface.
